title: Wire detect_sanitizer_ast via WALK-ONCE-INDEX
implemented: ADDED private fn build_sanitizer_ast_index(tree, source, language) -> HashMap<u32, SanitizerType> at crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:3553. Walks the tree ONCE, indexes sanitizer hits by line, applies is_in_comment + is_in_string filters. Mirrors detect_sanitizer_ast body but without the per-line line filter and the WALK-ONCE pattern at compute_taint_with_tree L3573-3590.

ADDED invocation of build_sanitizer_ast_index at top of compute_taint_with_tree (after build_refs_by_block); result stored in `sanitizer_ast_index: HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>`. Gated on `(tree, source)` like the source/sink walk; empty HashMap when tree is None.

EXTENDED process_block signature: added `sanitizer_ast_index: &HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` parameter (private API). Also DROPPED the unused `_line_to_block` parameter to keep argument count at 8 (passes clippy::too_many_arguments without #[allow]).
EXTENDED SsaPropagateCtx struct: added field `sanitizer_ast_index: &'a HashMap<u32, SanitizerType>` (private struct).

REPLACED detect_sanitizer call at process_block L4109 with AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK: `let ast_sanitizer_hit = sanitizer_ast_index.contains_key(&var_ref.line); if ast_sanitizer_hit || detect_sanitizer(stmt, language).is_some() { ... }`
REPLACED detect_sanitizer call at ssa_propagate L4358 with the analogous AST-FIRST gate using inst.line.
THREADED &sanitizer_ast_index through both call sites (process_block invocation + SsaPropagateCtx construction at compute_taint_with_tree L3700/L3823).
PRESERVED detect_sanitizer_ast public per-line API at L3498 (kept for external callers; not invoked from worklist).
PRESERVED detect_sanitizer regex function at L1096 (still active during M2 — regex fallback).
PRESERVED all 16 LanguagePatterns sanitizer Vecs and all 16 AST_SANITIZERS banks (M3 audit / M4 delete).

ast_only_harness.before: 0 passed / 18 failed at HEAD pre-M2 (all RED)
ast_only_harness.actual: 18 of 18 RED -> GREEN (exceeded threshold; the 2 pre-flagged parity-gap targets ruby_rack_utils_escape_html + php_int_cast also flipped GREEN at M2 since their AST patterns were already populated at HEAD)

preexisting_unrelated_failures: rr_module_function_integ_test::ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint (docstring confirms `fails under AST-only at HEAD pre-M2`; source-side AST gap, unrelated to sanitizer dispatch)

ast_tests, fs_tests, git_tests, language_parity_test, metrics_tests, quality_tests, val010_change_impact_git_path_test, bench_surface_search_multilang, doctest (kotlin/luau/surface) - pre-existing failures from other workstreams, no taint connection

loc_delta_actual.vs_contract_estimate_50: +31 overage attributable to inline doc comments (build_sanitizer_ast_index docblock + comment block on AST-FIRST-WITH-REGEX-FALLBACK at both call sites + SsaPropagateCtx field doc + compute_taint_with_tree wiring comment). Functional code is well under 50 LOC.

discoveries.title: cpp_std_stoi_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize regressed PASS -> FAIL at M2
discoveries.summary: At HEAD pre-M2, the cpp string-literal regression-guard PASSED because the C++ regex sanitizer bank's pattern was constrained enough that it did not match `std::stoi` text inside a string literal. Post-M2, the AST-FIRST gate also fires on this fixture because CPP_AST_SANITIZERS uses raw-substring pattern `("", "std::stoi")` and member_patterns_match's raw-substring fallback at taint.rs:3094-3098 matches the assignment node's text (which spans the string-literal child).
discoveries.root_cause: member_patterns_match raw-substring fallback walks `descendant_text` (output of node_text on the descendant). For an assignment node `let msg = "... std::stoi ...";` the text DOES contain `std::stoi`, but the descendant itself is NOT a string node so is_in_string returns false. The substring match fires. This is a latent defect in the AST API pre-existing in detect_sanitizer_ast — it was hidden because detect_sanitizer_ast was DEAD CODE at the worklist call sites at HEAD pre-M2.
discoveries.preexists: Yes — same defect exists in detect_sanitizer_ast (L3498) and the same pattern is used by detect_sources_ast / detect_sinks_ast for raw-substring fallbacks. Source/sink raw-substring usage is rarer (most patterns are call-shaped), which is why the issue surfaces specifically for sanitizers.
discoveries.scope: 1 of 16 string-literal regression-guards. The other 14 already FAILED at HEAD due to the regex bank — those remain FAILED at M2 (no change). Only cpp went the wrong direction (PASS -> FAIL).
discoveries.owner: M3 (AST sanitizer parity audit) or M4 (dispatch flip). M3 is the natural fit: tighten member_patterns_match's raw-substring fallback to skip descendants whose text contains string-literal children, OR replace ('', 'std::stoi') with a structural pattern in CPP_AST_SANITIZERS. Per stop_thresholds the contract expected 16 *_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize tests behavior 'preserved'; cpp regressed by 1, so this is documented for orchestrator decision.
discoveries.non_blocking_for_m2: Per dispatch-contract.json M2 success criteria (1) cargo check PASS, (2) 14 of 16 ast_only RED -> GREEN, (3) all 16 sanitize_breaks_flow_<lang> regular tests still GREEN, (4) string-literal class behavior preserved, (5) val/rr/security/taint_tests GREEN, (6) clippy -D warnings PASS — criteria 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 met; criterion 4 met for 14 of 15 (cpp regressed). Net win: 18 ast_only flipped GREEN vs 1 string-literal regression. Recommended to ship M2 and address cpp at M3 / M4.

discoveries.title: process_block had an unused parameter `_line_to_block`
discoveries.summary: Pre-existing dead parameter at process_block L4074 (`_line_to_block: &HashMap<u32, usize>`). The underscore prefix indicates the function does not consume it. Adding the new sanitizer_ast_index parameter would have brought the signature to 9 args, tripping clippy::too_many_arguments. Per the no-gaming rule (only `_` prefix when value is genuinely not needed), removing the unused parameter is the correct fix and keeps the signature at 8 args. Updated the single call site at compute_taint_with_tree.

compliance.no_blocks_no_push: OK (no push, no cargo publish, no version bump)
compliance.detect_sanitizer_ast_body_unchanged: OK (wrapped via new helper, not inlined)
